cataloging_subject.label: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses Enclosid with Appendix (rule-and-observance compilation)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The Middle English compilation delimited by the pinned U-M/HTI XML and Walter W. Seton's 1914 edition: the seventeen-division Isabella Rule at Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 585 Part II, fols. 48r-72r, followed by an Appendix comprising an unidentified papal document at fols. 72v-78v and convent-observance regulations at fols. 78v-101r. It is not one homogeneous medieval work, not the whole current composite codex, not Seton's complete Franciscan component or EETS volume, and not the sibling Third Order rule whose former Pennant/Seton witness is now NLW MS 22873B.

catalog_summary.title: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses Enclosid with Appendix (rule-and-observance compilation)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English translator; anonymous compiler(s) of the rule, papal fragment, and observance regulations; source rule confirmed by Urban IV and revised under Boniface VIII
catalog_summary.editor: Walter W. Seton (1914); manuscript transcript by Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes; R. W. Chambers assisted revision; U-M/HTI encoding (1999)
catalog_summary.date: Translation and compilation undated; first attested in Bodley 585 Part II, dated c. 1440-1460
catalog_summary.region: England; association of the witness with the London Minoresses is tentative

titles.value: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses Enclosid with Appendix (rule-and-observance compilation)
notes: The qualifier makes the exact three-unit boundary visible; neither the manuscript nor Seton gives the whole compilation one uniform medieval title.

titles.value: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses enclosid
notes: The title is the source-edition and electronic title for the main rule; the inspected fol. 48r plate begins with Urban's address and does not show this heading.

titles.value: Rewle of Sustris Menouresses enclosid / edited from a XVth century MS. (MS. Bodl. 585) in the Bodleian Library with an introduction, notes, and glossary by Walter W. Seton

agents.name: Boniface VIII, Pope

agents.name: R. W. Chambers
notes: Editor of the courtesy-book component of EETS OS 148; Seton separately thanks him for helping revise the Minoresses text.

agents.name: Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes

scope.description: Issuer and textual authority of the Latin Isabella Rule confirmed in Religionis augmentum

notes: Source-work responsibility only; this does not attribute the Middle English wording to Urban IV or identify the translator's physical exemplar.

scope.description: Revision and approval of the source rule represented by the Middle English version

assertion.notes: The witness names Boniface VIII in its profession formula, but the exact textual extent and date of his revision were not established.

notes: Seton judges that the English was probably translated through a French version of the Latin rule, but no intermediate text, translator, date, or place is identified.

scope.description: Assembly of the main rule, papal document, and convent-observance regulations represented by the same-hand Bodley manuscript unit

notes: The contiguous same-hand copy confirms compilation at the witness layer, but does not establish whether its scribe assembled the documents or copied a pre-existing compilation.

This dates the surviving copy, not the source rule, translation, or Appendix documents.
The current catalog assigns English origin to the manuscript part; it does not identify a more precise copying or translation place.

language_statements.label: Middle English
notes: The XML header omits Middle English even though it is overwhelmingly the primary language.

scope.description: Three tagged prayer formulae in the main rule and 191 tagged liturgical formulae, incipits, responses, and office terms in Appendix sections 2-10

language_statements.label: Ancient Greek

scope.description: Four transliterated Kyrie/Christe eleison formulae tagged LANG=gre in the liturgical observance regulations

notes: The normalized language code is grc; the XML's legacy gre value is retained only as source data. The phrases are transliterated in Latin script.

form_statements.description: All twenty-seven encoded divisions are prose. Embedded Latin and transliterated Greek liturgical formulae do not create verse items; the XML contains no L or LG elements.

genre_statements.label: Religious rule and observance compilation
notes: The whole-scope term preserves the rule-plus-Appendix boundary without treating all three documents as one genre.

genre_statements.label: Vernacular religious translation
notes: The translation relation to the Isabella Rule is secure; Seton's proposed French intermediary is probable rather than identified.

assertion.notes: Seton identifies the form as a portion of a papal bull confirming or modifying the rule but could not identify the bull.

genre_statements.label: Monastic customary
notes: A modern genre label for the regulations governing bells, choir, Mass, refectory rites, feasts, and the liturgical calendar.

subject_statements.label: Religious life of enclosed Minoresses (Poor Clares)
Admission, profession, visitation, discipline, and community governance
Divine Office, choir, Mass, communion, refectory rites, and the liturgical year

summaries.value: A mid-fifteenth-century Middle English rule-and-observance compilation for enclosed Minoresses. Its main text translates the Isabella Rule confirmed by Urban IV and subsequently revised under Boniface VIII; it regulates profession, enclosure, clothing, worship, fasting, communication, sacramental care, offices, visitation, and correction. A following papal document confirms or modifies observance, while nine further sections prescribe bells, choir and Mass practice, communion, refectory blessings, feasts, and the liturgical calendar.

The main rule occupies fols. 48r-72r and has seventeen U-M-supplied chapter divisions. Appendix section 1 occupies fols. 72v-78v and ends with 'without any variaunce or lettinge'; Seton identifies it as part of an otherwise untraced papal document. Appendix sections 2-10 occupy fols. 78v-101r and form a distinct observance customary. The final trailer declares the good work complete.

The current codex is composite: unrelated Latin St Albans material occupies Part I, fols. 1-47, and the cataloged Middle English unit is Part II. Seton prints the compilation at pp. 81-116 and reproduces only fol. 48r as a manuscript plate. The U-M encoding retains a component title page and the full rule-plus-Appendix text but omits Seton's introduction, bibliography, editorial note, plate, endnotes, and shared glossary.

extent.description: The encoded content has two DIV1 wrappers and twenty-seven DIV2 divisions: seventeen main-rule chapters and ten Appendix sections. Their numeric labels are U-M-supplied analytic headings, not transcribed medieval rubrics. The BODY contains 59 P elements; the file-wide count of 65 P includes six header paragraphs. It also has 36 print-page milestones (81-116), 107 manuscript leaf-side milestones (48r-101r), 194 Latin and four transliterated Greek FOREIGN elements, eight NOTE1, 21 CORR, 138 SUPPLIED, one ADD, eleven DEL, one UNCLEAR, and no L or LG elements. These are representation metrics, not proof that the source documents were originally numbered in twenty-seven sections.

content_parts.label: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses enclosid
content_parts.summary: A seventeen-division Middle English version of the Isabella Rule, revised under Boniface VIII, regulating enclosed religious life from profession through governance and visitation.
notes: Each division begins with a decorated or penwork initial in the manuscript according to Seton; the electronic Chapter 1-17 headings are supplied.

content_parts.label: Appendix
content_parts.summary: A same-hand continuation comprising an unidentified papal document followed by a distinct body of convent-observance regulations.
notes: Appendix is Seton's and U-M's presentation label. Seton explicitly warns that its contents are not one document.

content_parts.label: Appendix section 1: unidentified papal document
content_parts.summary: A Middle English portion of a papal document confirming or modifying the rule, including governance by Friars Minor, common observance, property and exemption provisions, profession, dormitory practice, and enclosure.
notes: A reference to Boniface VIII as predecessor proves only that the document is later than his pontificate; Seton found no identification in Potthast or Sbaralea.

content_parts.label: Appendix sections 2-10: convent-observance regulations
content_parts.summary: Nine sections prescribing bell-ringing, assembly, choir and Mass order, refectory reading and silence, communion, feast observance, table blessings, Holy Week and seasonal rites, and precedence in the liturgical calendar.
notes: Seton treats the shift at fol. 78v ('At alle þe houres') as a document boundary: the following material is not in papal-bull form and later uses first-person plural.

related_works.title: The Isabella Rule (Religionis augmentum), as revised under Boniface VIII
notes: This relation applies only to part:main-rule. Urban IV confirmed the Longchamp or Isabella Rule on 27 July 1263; the English witness represents a later Boniface VIII revision. Seton considers a French intermediary probable, but no physical source exemplar or intermediate version is identified.

evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT title page; BODY main text and Appendix; TRAILER
evidence.summary: Identifies U-M item RuleMinoresses, Seton's 1914 source edition, Bodley 585 on the encoded title page, 1999 OCR production, omitted apparatus, editorial tag practices, provider terms, manuscript and print milestones, and the complete encoded rule-plus-Appendix text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME item RuleMinoresses.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights statement, full-text action, and contents
evidence.summary: Confirms the discovery title, Seton editorship, London 1914 publication, stable handle, public HTML text, seventeen chapters plus ten Appendix sections, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: R. W. Chambers and Walter W. Seton, eds., A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, EETS Original Series 148 (London, 1914), Internet Archive courtesybook00unknuoft.
evidence.locator: Seton's introduction and bibliography pp. 61-78; editorial note; fol. 48r plate at PDF p. 98 / IA n97; text pp. 81-116 at PDF pp. 99-134 / IA n98-n133; notes pp. 117-125; shared glossary pp. 126-127
evidence.summary: The inspected complete scan establishes component boundaries and responsibility, the Isabella Rule source history, Bodley manuscript description and proposed provenance, Appendix document boundary, translation assessment, editorial conventions, full text, one selected witness plate, notes, and glossary.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Western Medieval Manuscripts, MS. Bodl. 585, pinned catalog TEI at medieval-mss commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.locator: msDesc MS_Bodl_585; msPart MS_Bodl_585-part2; msItem MS_Bodl_585-part2-item1
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark and identifiers; the two-part composite codex; Part II's Middle English Rule of St Clare item, IPMEP 797, parchment support, c. 1440-1460 English origin, penwork, Charles Howard's 1604 donation, and tentative contents-based association with the London Minoresses.
notes: The public catalog page was protected by an anti-bot interstitial during automated review; the exact official catalog source was verified and cached from Bodleian's pinned public TEI repository.

evidence.citation: XPath, element-count, and boundary inspection of repository RuleMinoresses.xml performed 2026-07-15.
evidence.locator: TEXT/BODY DIV1 RuleMinoresses:1-2 and DIV2 descendants
evidence.summary: Counts two BODY DIV1, twenty-seven DIV2, 59 BODY P and 65 file-wide P, 36 PB, 107 leaf MILESTONE, no L or LG, 194 Latin and four Greek FOREIGN, eight NOTE1, 21 CORR, 138 SUPPLIED, one ADD, eleven DEL, and one UNCLEAR; confirms the main 48r-72r and two Appendix 72v-78v / 78v-101r boundaries.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/3rdFranRule.xml, inspected solely to resolve the RuleMinoresses header's inherited witness note.
evidence.locator: HEADER SOURCEDESC; FRONT title page; manuscript folio milestones
evidence.summary: Shows that 'olim Seton, olim Pennant' is the source-witness designation for the sibling Third Order rule. Together with its current lineage record and Seton's separate component title pages, this excludes the Pennant/NLW witness from RuleMinoresses, whose own title page names Bodley 585.

evidence.citation: General Corpus lineage manifest for RuleMinoresses.
evidence.summary: Binds the work record to Bodley 585 Part II, its current composite host, Seton's edition and single fol. 48r plate, U-M's 1999 encoding, the local XML artifact, the complete 1914 book scan, and the Isabella Rule source context.

open_questions.question: When, where, by whom, and from which exact French or Latin exemplar were the Middle English rule and its compilation with the Appendix produced?
next_steps: Consult current linguistic, paleographical, and codicological scholarship and inspect the complete manuscript.
Do not turn the witness date, English origin, or tentative London association into a precise translation or compilation date and place.

open_questions.question: What are the exact source, date, issuer, authorship, and transmission history of the two Appendix components?
next_steps: Search current papal-register and Franciscan-rule corpora for Appendix section 1 beyond Seton's Potthast and Sbaralea checks.
Compare sections 2-10 with Poor Clare and Friars Minor customaries, ordinals, calendars, and table-grace traditions.
Preserve the fol. 78v document boundary unless stronger evidence shows further subdivisions or common authorship.

open_questions.question: Was MS. Bodl. 585 Part II copied for or used by the London Minoresses outside Aldgate?
next_steps: Retain the current Bodleian/MLGB3 question mark and seek direct codicological, liturgical, obit, or archival evidence beyond general fit of contents.
Review later provenance scholarship before repeating Seton's near-certain assignment or his inferred post-Dissolution ownership chain.

open_questions.question: Do the twenty-seven supplied U-M divisions exactly match every intellectual break and decorated initial in MS. Bodl. 585 Part II?
next_steps: Inspect a complete manuscript surrogate or the physical codex against Seton's layout and U-M's supplied chapter and section headings.
Keep the twenty-seven count explicitly representation-level until that comparison is possible.

notes: The XML TITLE TYPE=130 value 'Fifteenth-century courtesy book. Selections' belongs to the composite 1914 carrier and is not a medieval title for this compilation.

The XML SOURCEDESC note 'Derived from olim Seton, olim Pennant' is inherited from sibling 3rdFranRule and is contradicted for this item by its own Bodley 585 title page, Seton's separate source discussion, and the current Bodleian catalog. No Pennant or NLW witness edge is asserted here.
The XML language profile declares Latin and Greek but omits primary Middle English; the manifest corrects the profile from direct text and current catalog inspection and normalizes legacy gre to grc.
R. W. Chambers edited the courtesy-book component of EETS OS 148, not the Minoresses component; Seton edited the latter, Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes transcribed it, and Chambers assisted revision.

Copyright, provider terms, physical and reproduction access, the single public fol. 48r plate, the negative complete-facsimile search, and the U-M terms conflict are recorded in the linked lineage manifest.
